commands.observed: RUN: plan-002-tests: cwd=. argv=['uv', 'run', 'pytest', 'tests/forkproof/forkpoints', '-q', '-m', 'not integration']; 8 passed, 1 deselected in 0.03s

RUN: integration-forkpoint: cwd=. argv=['uv', 'run', 'pytest', 'tests/forkproof/forkpoints', '-q', '-m', 'integration']; 1 passed, 8 deselected in 71.22s. This exports the real HUD trace boundary, runs a real Modal Filesystem Snapshot round-trip over task-relevant paths, replays 29 accepted trace tool calls into a fresh Modal Mongo sandbox, then creates/restores an orchestrated live-boundary ForkPoint, persists its immutable record, and verifies negative restore rejection cases without provider fallback.
RUN: lint: cwd=. argv=['uv', 'run', 'ruff', 'check', 'docs/plans/scripts']; All checks passed!
PASS: 20 text file(s) are at most 500 lines
FAIL: 4 validation error(s): status must be complete, completed_at is required, complete manifest cannot have blockers, and commands[4] did not exit 0.

checks.name: Plan 002 command routing
checks.evidence: docs/plans/repo-map/COMMANDS.json now maps plan-002-tests and integration-forkpoint to verified pytest argv with PYTHONPATH=src under a narrow documented ownership exception.

checks.name: Six behavior cases
checks.evidence: tests/forkproof/forkpoints/test_core.py covers valid capture, valid restore, boundary mismatch, history mismatch, grader mismatch, unsupported mode, partial cleanup, and finalized-record immutability against the repo-native contract with a deterministic local provider.

checks.name: Integration readiness
checks.evidence: tests/forkproof/forkpoints/test_integration_readiness.py runs forkproof.forkpoints.integration_probe, which exports the accepted HUD trace into a redacted boundary summary, runs a real Modal Filesystem Snapshot round-trip over /app, /data/db, and /var/log/mongodb.log markers, replays accepted trace tool calls, creates/restores an orchestrated live-boundary ForkPoint through the repo-native contract, persists docs/plans/evidence/002/artifacts/forkpoint-record.json, and writes docs/plans/evidence/002/artifacts/integration-readiness.json.

checks.name: Real HUD/Modal ForkPoint completion
checks.evidence: Not complete: evidence now has a redacted HUD trace boundary export, a real Modal Filesystem Snapshot round-trip, replay-derived snapshot/restore evidence, an orchestrated live-boundary ForkPoint created from a retained Modal sandbox handle, deterministic environment source-tree digest evidence, and restored-sandbox negative security probes. The already-finished historical HUD trace still has no live executable sandbox handle attached, the environment digest is not a registry manifest digest, and full branch security proof remains outside Plan 002.

checks.name: Orchestrated live-boundary ForkPoint
checks.evidence: docs/plans/evidence/002/artifacts/live-boundary-forkpoint.json records fork point fp-f1b3d319693c089ff4a63c4b, Modal Filesystem Snapshot im-01KVKQN7WAMWCJGCGF3D1X9RXZ, boundary token/history hash, source-tree image digest, MongoDB fsync quiescence, query hash equality after restore, grader-output hash after restore, and block_network/no-secrets/resource labels with /root/.env, Docker socket, and metadata network negative probes. It also records boundary, history, grader, and unsupported-mode restore rejections with no provider restore fallback. docs/plans/evidence/002/artifacts/forkpoint-record.json persists the immutable ForkPoint record with content digest 64bd78eb22b18e838f50f0ff376455b9a89fc8c2dfd562de082b7475c8b29f71.

artifacts.description: Blocked Plan 002 evidence manifest recording the precondition failure and exact Done-when command observations.
Living-doc log updated with the Plan 002 STOP.
Plan 002 ForkPoint capture/restore contract, immutable JSON store, deterministic local snapshot provider, and integration-readiness command.
Behavior tests and integration-readiness test for the Plan 002 ForkPoint feature.
Integration-readiness artifact naming the local-provider ForkPoint id, Plan 003 handoff keys, and remaining real-system gaps.
Redacted accepted HUD trace export: event count, event-stream digests, selected final scenario-evaluate boundary, and hashed tail event metadata.
Real Modal Filesystem Snapshot round-trip over task-relevant marker paths with block_network, no secrets, resource labels, and prohibited mount checks.
Replay-derived Modal Filesystem Snapshot from accepted HUD trace tool calls: replay count, skipped failed calls, snapshot id, query.py hash, grader-output hashes, and policy labels.

Orchestrated accepted-trace rerun ForkPoint from a retained Modal sandbox handle: ForkPoint id, boundary token, history hash, source-tree image digest, Filesystem Snapshot id, restore handoff keys, MongoDB quiescence, query hash, grader hash, policy labels, and restored-sandbox negative security probes.

Persisted immutable ForkPoint record reloaded from the repo-native store: required interface fields, content digest, source evidence refs, snapshot identity, history hash, environment identity, and policy labels.

skips.name: Atomic source-sandbox boundary capture
skips.reason: The accepted HUD trace can now be exported, redacted, replayed, and captured from a retained Modal sandbox handle during an orchestrated rerun. There is still no provider handle to the already-finished historical sandbox instance from the original HUD run.

skips.name: Full branch security proof
skips.reason: The Modal probes record block_network, no secrets, resource labels, prohibited mount checks, absent /root/.env, absent Docker socket, and blocked metadata network. Full adversarial branch isolation remains Plan 003-owned.

blockers.name: No live executable state for accepted historical boundary
blockers.evidence: docs/plans/evidence/002/artifacts/live-boundary-forkpoint.json proves an orchestrated accepted-trace rerun can pause with a retained Modal sandbox handle, create a repo-native ForkPoint, snapshot the filesystem, restore it, and run task-visible and negative security probes. It is still not the already-finished historical sandbox instance from the original HUD run; the environment image identity is a deterministic source-tree digest rather than a registry manifest digest; and full adversarial branch security proof remains outside Plan 002.
blockers.required_next_step: Either accept the orchestrated rerun as the canonical source ForkPoint for later plans and update the Plan 002 completion criteria accordingly, or obtain provider-level access to the original historical sandbox instance. Also bind a registry image manifest digest or explicitly accept the source-tree digest as sufficient, and complete the full branch security proof before setting the manifest complete.

notes: The previous COMMANDS.json start blocker is unblocked in this PR under a user-approved narrow ownership exception.
No final real ForkPoint, Modal snapshot, or restore fidelity completion claim is made from this in-progress manifest.
